steps.title: Factor the left side
steps.content:
Notice that the first two terms share the factor $(9x - 2a)$:

$$x(9x - 2a) + b(9x - 2a) - (x + b) = 0$$

Factor out $(9x - 2a)$:

$$(9x - 2a)(x + b) - (x + b) = 0$$

Now factor out $(x + b)$:

$$(x + b)(9x - 2a - 1) = 0$$

steps.title: Identify which factor gives x = 42
steps.content:
The factored form gives two solutions. Either $x + b = 0$, so $x = -b$ (negative since $b > 0$), or $9x - 2a - 1 = 0$, so $x = \frac{2a + 1}{9}$. Since $x = 42$ is a positive solution, it must satisfy the second equation:

$$\frac{2a + 1}{9} = 42$$
